m1.title: Market Foundations
m1.desc: Understand how the stock market works, primary vs secondary markets, and what it means to own a share.
m1.theory:
The stock market is the centralized marketplace where shares of publicly traded companies are exchanged. It consists of the Primary Market (IPOs) where companies raise capital, and the Secondary Market (Exchanges) where investors trade with each other.

KEY CONCEPTS
• STOCK: Represents ownership fraction in a company.
• EXCHANGE: Marketplace where stocks are bought and sold.
• PRICE: Determined by supply and demand.
• VOLUME: Number of shares traded.

PRIMARY VS SECONDARY MARKET
• PRIMARY MARKET: Companies issue new shares to raise capital. Example: Initial Public Offerings (IPOs).
• SECONDARY MARKET: Investors trade existing shares. Example: The BVC.

FUN FACTS
• The first stock exchange was founded in Amsterdam in 1602.
• The NYSE (New York Stock Exchange) is the largest in the world.
• Apple is the most valuable company in the world by market cap.

quiz.q1: Where do investors trade existing shares with each other?

m1.simulation: Goal: Explore the Market and identify a high-volume stock to understand supply and demand.

m2.title: The Intelligent Investor
m2.desc: Learn the principles of value investing from Benjamin Graham: Margin of Safety and Mr. Market.
m2.theory:
Benjamin Graham, considered the father of fundamental analysis, taught the Value Investment philosophy. His book The Intelligent Investor remains an essential reference.

FUNDAMENTAL CONCEPTS
• VALUE INVESTING: Buying stocks at a price below their intrinsic value.
• INTRINSIC VALUE: The real value of a company based on its fundamentals.
• MARGIN OF SAFETY: Difference between market price and intrinsic value.

MR. MARKET
• An allegory representing the emotional swings of the market.
• A value investor stays calm during downturns.
• Takes advantage of market panic to buy opportunities.

FUN FACTS
• Warren Buffett was a student of Benjamin Graham.
• The value philosophy has generated average returns of 15-20% per year.
• Graham died in 1976, but his ideas remain relevant.

quiz.q1: The practice of buying a stock significantly below its intrinsic value is called:

m2.simulation: Goal: Find a stable stock and buy $500 worth of shares to practice long-term holding.

m3.title: Portfolio Strategy
m3.desc: Master diversification and understand your risk tolerance.
m3.theory:
Diversification is the practice of spreading investments across different assets to reduce risk. It is one of the most important concepts in investing.

TYPES OF INVESTORS
• DEFENSIVE INVESTOR: Prefers established companies with dividends. Less risk, less potential return.
• ENTERPRISING INVESTOR: Seeks high-growth opportunities. Higher risk, higher potential return.

DIVERSIFICATION
• Don't put all your eggs in one basket.
• Invest in different sectors: technology, healthcare, consumer, finance.
• Consider different geographies: Colombia, USA, emerging markets.

RISK MANAGEMENT
• Define your risk tolerance before investing.
• Don't invest what you can't afford to lose.
• Review your portfolio regularly.

FUN FACTS
• The saying "don't put all your eggs in one basket" has Jewish origins.
• The 2020-2022 period taught the importance of diversification.

quiz.q1: What is the main benefit of diversification?

m3.simulation: Goal: Buy stocks from two different sectors (e.g., Tech and Retail) to diversify your risk.

m4.title: Brokers in Colombia
m4.desc: Learn all the local stock brokers to trade on the BVC and find the best one for you.
m4.theory:
A broker or stock broker is an entity authorized by the Colombian Financial Superintendency to act as an intermediary in the buying and selling of securities on the Bogotá Stock Exchange (BVC). Brokers are essential because individual investors cannot trade directly on the exchange: they need an authorized intermediary.

TYPES OF BROKERS
• INTEGRAL BROKERS: The most common, offer complete custody, trading, and advisory services. Examples: Serfinco, Correval, Scotia Valores.
• AUTHORIZED BROKERS: Can trade on the BVC but offer more limited services. Examples: Alianza Valores, Casa de Bolsa.

MAIN BROKERS
• SERFINCO: One of the most popular platforms, founded in 1962, known for its intuitive platform and competitive commissions.
• CORREVAL: Part of the Bolívar Group, comprehensive services with national presence.
• SCOTIA VALORES COLOMBIA: Part of Scotiabank, services for all investor levels.
• ALIANZA VALORES: Broker focused on personalized advisory.
• CASA DE BOLSA: Subsidiary of Grupo Aval, custody and trading services.
• BVC: In addition to being the exchange, offers brokerage services directly.

HOW TO OPEN AN ACCOUNT
• Choose a broker that fits your investor profile.
• Gather documents: ID, income certificate, bank statements.
• Complete account opening form.
• Deposit minimum amount (varies by broker, from 500,000 COP).
• Wait for validation and activation (1-5 business days).

TYPICAL COMMISSIONS
• Trading commission: 0.1% - 0.5% of transaction value.
• Custody: 0 - 25,000 COP monthly.
• Withdrawals: 0 - 15,000 COP.

FUN FACTS
• The BVC was founded in 2001, merging the stock exchanges of Bogotá, Medellín and Barranquilla.
• Colombia is the third largest stock exchange in Latin America after Brazil and Mexico.
• You can start investing with only 500,000 COP.
• Trading hours at the BVC are from 9:00 AM to 4:00 PM Colombia time.

quiz.q1: What entity authorizes brokers to operate in Colombia?

m4.simulation: Goal: Explore the platforms of different brokers and compare their commissions and services.

m5.title: Cryptocurrencies
m5.desc: Understand the world of cryptocurrencies, blockchain, and how they differ from traditional stocks.
m5.theory:
Cryptocurrencies are decentralized digital currencies that use cryptography to secure transactions and control the creation of new units. Unlike stocks, they are not backed by a company or grant ownership rights.

HOW BLOCKCHAIN WORKS
• Decentralization: No central authority controls the network.
• Immutability: Once recorded, a transaction cannot be modified.
• Transparency: Anyone can verify transactions on the public chain.

THE TWO MAIN CRYPTOCURRENCIES
• BITCOIN (BTC): Released in 2009 by Satoshi Nakamoto. Maximum of 21 million bitcoins. Uses proof of work (PoW).
• ETHEREUM (ETH): Released in 2015 by Vitalik Buterin. Enables smart contracts. Migrating to proof of stake (PoS).

TYPES OF WALLETS
• HOT WALLETS: Connected to the internet, more convenient but less secure. Examples: Binance, Coinbase.
• COLD WALLETS: Offline, more secure. Examples: Ledger, Trezor.

REGULATION IN COLOMBIA
• DIAN has established that cryptocurrencies are not legal tender in Colombia.
• Transactions with cryptocurrencies may have tax implications.
• There is currently no clear regulatory framework for cryptocurrency exchanges.

FUN FACTS
• The first real payment with Bitcoin was in 2010: 10,000 BTC for 2 pizzas.
• At its peak, Bitcoin reached almost 69,000 USD.
• There are more than 10,000 cryptocurrencies, but most have little value.

RISKS TO CONSIDER
• Extreme volatility: Prices can move 20%+ in a single day.
• No protection: No guarantee fund like bank deposits.
• Scams: Hackers, phishing, rug pulls.

quiz.q1: What was the first real thing bought with Bitcoin?

m5.simulation: Goal: Explore the Bitcoin price chart and observe its historical volatility.

m6.title: International Finance
m6.desc: Discover global markets, ADRs, Forex, and how to invest around the world.
m6.theory:
International finance encompasses all instruments, markets, and participants operating beyond a country's borders. Investing globally allows for risk diversification and access to unique opportunities.

THE MAIN GLOBAL MARKETS
• NYSE (NEW YORK): The largest stock exchange in the world. More than 2,400 listed companies. Hours: 9:30 AM - 4:00 PM EST.
• NASDAQ (NEW YORK): Center of technology and innovation. Apple, Amazon, Google, Tesla. Hours: 9:30 AM - 4:00 PM EST.
• LONDON STOCK EXCHANGE (LSE): The sixth largest in the world. Historic since 1688. Hours: 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM GMT.
• TOKYO STOCK EXCHANGE (JPX): Main market in Asia. Toyota, Sony, SoftBank. Hours: 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM JST.
• HONG KONG STOCK EXCHANGE (HKEX): Gateway to China. Alibaba, Tencent. Hours: 9:30 AM - 4:00 PM HKT.

ADRs (AMERICAN DEPOSITARY RECEIPTS)
• ADRs are certificates representing shares of foreign companies.
• Trade in US dollars on the NYSE or NASDAQ.
• Each ADR may represent 1, 2, 4 or more shares of the original company.
• Popular examples: AAPL (Apple), GOOGL (Google), MSFT (Microsoft), AMZN (Amazon), TSLA (Tesla).

FOREX (FOREIGN EXCHANGE MARKET)
• The foreign exchange market is the largest in the world.
• Daily volume: More than 7.5 trillion dollars.
• Operates 24 hours, 5 days a week.
• Major pairs: EUR/USD, GBP/USD, USD/JPY.
• The EUR/USD pair represents approximately 85% of total volume.

EXCHANGE RATES AND COLOMBIA
• The Colombian peso (COP) is relatively stable compared to other currencies in the region.
• The US dollar is the most traded currency against the COP.
• Factors affecting the exchange rate: oil prices, investment flows, Banco de la República policies.

FUN FACTS
• The Amsterdam Stock Exchange is the world's oldest market, founded in 1602.
• The NYSE was created in 1817, originally was a group of brokers under a tree.
• In 1987, Black Monday, the Dow Jones fell 22.6% in a single day.
• The market cap of Apple, Microsoft, and Alphabet exceeds the GDP of most countries.
• The Forex market operates 24 hours because when New York closes, Tokyo opens.

quiz.q1: What is the largest currency market in the world by daily volume?

m6.simulation: Goal: Explore an ADR (like AAPL or GOOGL) and understand how to invest in global companies.
